title: Google Q2 Capital Expenditure Doubles to Record High: $44.9 Billion Invested in AI Infrastructure, Cloud Business Profit Margin Almost Doubles
excerpt: Google's computing power bill is rising at an alarming rate. Alphabet's second-quarter financial report shows that capital expenditures jumped 100% year-over-ye
content_html:
Google's computing power bill is rising at an alarming rate. Alphabet's second-quarter financial report shows that capital expenditures jumped 100% year-over-year, reaching a record $44.92 billion. At this pace, the annualized spending is already approaching $180 billion. But the real story is the ever-accelerating money-making machine behind this expenditure.

Google's revenue in the second quarter grew 24% year-over-year, reaching $119.8 billion, exceeding analysts' estimates of about $116.9 billion. The most impressive was Google Cloud — revenue surged 82% to $24.8 billion, with operating profit margin nearly doubling to 35.6%, and backlogs have now reached $514 billion. Gemini also delivered progress: monthly active users reached 950 million, and the model API processes as many as 22 billion tokens per minute. As cloud business handles massive demand while expanding profit margins, it is the direct confidence behind Google's decision to double its capital spending.

This wave of AI infrastructure isn't limited to Google alone. Morgan Stanley predicts that capital expenditures by hyper-scale cloud service providers will jump from around $800 billion in 2026 to $1.2 trillion in 2027; Goldman Sachs estimates $76.5 billion for 2026, rising to $1.6 trillion by 2031. McKinsey's projection is even more dramatic — the total capital expenditures related to this will reach $6.7 trillion by 2030, with data centers focused on AI alone absorbing $5.2 trillion. When a company can double its quarterly capex and simultaneously double its cloud profit margin, it's clearly not betting on returns from just one or two quarters, but rather on an entire infrastructure cycle rewritten by AI.

sentences.en: Google's computing power bill is rising at an alarming rate.
sentences.zh: 谷歌的计算能力费用正在以惊人的速度增长。

sentences.en: Alphabet's second-quarter financial report shows that capital expenditures jumped 100% year-over-year, reaching a record $44.92 billion.
sentences.zh: Alphabet第二季度财报显示，资本支出同比猛增100%，达到创纪录的449.2亿美元。

sentences.en: At this pace, the annualized spending is already approaching $180 billion.
sentences.zh: 按照这个速度，年化支出已经接近 1800 亿美元。

sentences.en: But the real story is the ever-accelerating money-making machine behind this expenditure.
sentences.zh: 但真正的故事是这笔支出背后不断加速的赚钱机器。

sentences.en: Google's revenue in the second quarter grew 24% year-over-year, reaching $119.8 billion, exceeding analysts' estimates of about $116.9 billion.
sentences.zh: 谷歌第二季度营收同比增长24%，达到1198亿美元，超出分析师约1169亿美元的预期。

sentences.en: The most impressive was Google Cloud — revenue surged 82% to $24.8 billion, with operating profit margin nearly doubling to 35.6%, and backlogs have now reached $514 billion.

sentences.en: Gemini also delivered progress: monthly active users reached 950 million, and the model API processes as many as 22 billion tokens per minute.
sentences.zh: Gemini 也取得了进展：月活跃用户达到 9.5 亿，模型 API 每分钟处理多达 220 亿个代币。

sentences.zh: 由于云业务在处理大量需求的同时扩大了利润率，这是谷歌决定将资本支出增加一倍的直接信心。

sentences.en: This wave of AI infrastructure isn't limited to Google alone.
sentences.zh: 这波人工智能基础设施浪潮并不仅限于谷歌。

sentences.en: Morgan Stanley predicts that capital expenditures by hyper-scale cloud service providers will jump from around $800 billion in 2026 to $1.2 trillion in 2027; Goldman Sachs estimates $76.5 billion for 2026, rising to $1.6 trillion by 2031.
sentences.zh: 摩根士丹利预测，超大规模云服务提供商的资本支出将从 2026 年的约 8000 亿美元跃升至 2027 年的 1.2 万亿美元；高盛预计 2026 年将达到 765 亿美元，到 2031 年将增至 1.6 万亿美元。

sentences.en: McKinsey's projection is even more dramatic — the total capital expenditures related to this will reach $6.7 trillion by 2030, with data centers focused on AI alone absorbing $5.2 trillion.
sentences.zh: 麦肯锡的预测更加引人注目——到 2030 年，与此相关的总资本支出将达到 6.7 万亿美元，其中仅专注于人工智能的数据中心就吸收了 5.2 万亿美元。

sentences.en: When a company can double its quarterly capex and simultaneously double its cloud profit margin, it's clearly not betting on returns from just one or two quarters, but rather on an entire infrastructure cycle rewritten by AI.
sentences.zh: 当一家公司能够将季度资本支出增加一倍，同时将云利润率增加一倍时，它显然不是押注于一两个季度的回报，而是押注于人工智能重写的整个基础设施周期。

content_html_zh:
谷歌的计算能力费用正在以惊人的速度增长。 Alphabet第二季度财报显示，资本支出同比猛增100%，达到创纪录的449.2亿美元。 按照这个速度，年化支出已经接近 1800 亿美元。 但真正的故事是这笔支出背后不断加速的赚钱机器。

谷歌第二季度营收同比增长24%，达到1198亿美元，超出分析师约1169亿美元的预期。 最令人印象深刻的是谷歌云——收入飙升82%，达到248亿美元，营业利润率几乎翻倍，达到35.6%，积压订单现已达到5140亿美元。 Gemini 也取得了进展：月活跃用户达到 9.5 亿，模型 API 每分钟处理多达 220 亿个代币。 由于云业务在处理大量需求的同时扩大了利润率，这是谷歌决定将资本支出增加一倍的直接信心。

这波人工智能基础设施浪潮并不仅限于谷歌。 摩根士丹利预测，超大规模云服务提供商的资本支出将从 2026 年的约 8000 亿美元跃升至 2027 年的 1.2 万亿美元；高盛预计 2026 年将达到 765 亿美元，到 2031 年将增至 1.6 万亿美元。 麦肯锡的预测更加引人注目——到 2030 年，与此相关的总资本支出将达到 6.7 万亿美元，其中仅专注于人工智能的数据中心就吸收了 5.2 万亿美元。 当一家公司能够将季度资本支出增加一倍，同时将云利润率增加一倍时，它显然不是押注于一两个季度的回报，而是押注于人工智能重写的整个基础设施周期。
